pages.raw_text:
Just as was anticipated, the
Great Northern has absolutely re-
fused to aid Prosecuting Attorney
F. C. Pugh in his efforts to bring to
light the relations of Judge M. j.
Gordon, former counsel for the
Great Northern, and Judge Milo A.
Root, former justice of the supreme
court of the state. 1t has likewise
refused to throw any light on the
alleged shortage of Judge Gordon
with the company, which people in
the confidence of the railroad esti-
mated to be as much as $4,000.
And worse still, it has even refused
to lend it's aid to the state In the
prosecution of the case against
Gordon for the Sparrow shortage OF
$4,000, for which Gordon was ar
rested and is under bond of $780,
900.


But the prosecutor is not yet
through with the Great Northern.
Since the big corporation has con-
cluded to stand on its dignity and
refuse to aid in the prosecution of
Judge Gordon, it is quite likely that
the prosecutor Office of Spokane
county may have surprise or two
in store for Jim Hills road before
they get through with it.


What steps will be taken to bring
the Great Northern to time Will not
be given out just now, but some
thing is brewing. Prosecutor Pugh
thinks he has enough.
